name: Dialog
purpose: Interrupts the user with important information or status, requiring an acknowledgment or decision.
whenToUse: Reporting an error that blocks the user's flow (error variant)
Warning the user before a potentially harmful action (warning variant)
Displaying critical information requiring explicit acknowledgment (info variant)
whenNotToUse: Non-critical notifications — use Snackbar or inline messages instead
Complex forms or multi-step processes — use BottomSheet or a full screen instead
anatomy: Action row: 1-button (full-width) or 2-button (Cancel | OK, split with vertical divider)

actionLayouts.singleAction: Full-width confirm button (e.g. Done, OK) — used for info/success/warning acknowledgments
actionLayouts.twoAction: Cancel (left, tertiary text color) | Confirm (right, primary text color) — used for error / critical decisions

accessibility.focus: Focus should automatically move to the dialog confirm action when opened.

flutterMappingNotes: Uses a custom Column layout (not AlertDialog) to match the Figma flat-action-row pattern.
Dialog width is fixed at 300 dp — insetPadding is calculated from screen width.
Actions are plain Material InkWell targets (not HumaxButton) to avoid padding/shape interference.
VerticalDivider between two actions; Container(height:1) for the horizontal divider above actions.
barrierDismissible is always false per Moni spec.

exampleUsage.singleAction: HumaxDialog.show(context: context, variant: HumaxDialogVariant.success, icon: Icon(Icons.check_circle, size: 32), title: 'Success', content: 'Device registered.', confirmLabel: 'Done')
exampleUsage.twoAction: HumaxDialog.show(context: context, variant: HumaxDialogVariant.error, icon: Icon(Icons.error, size: 32), title: 'Sign In Error', content: 'Registered device limit reached.', confirmLabel: 'OK', cancelLabel: 'Cancel', onConfirm: handleOk)
